## Filename: PMC11158672.md

**Title:** Influence of ABCB1 and ABCG2 polymorphisms on doxorubicin disposition in Asian breast cancer patients

**Authors:** Suman Lal, Zee Wan Wong, Edwin Sandanaraj, Xiaoqiang Xiang, Peter Cher Siang Ang, Edmund J D Lee, Balram Chowbay
**Journal:** Cancer Science
**Date:** 2008 Mar 28
**DOI:** 10.1111/j.1349-7006.2008.00744.x
**PMID:** 18377430
**PMCID:** PMC11158672

**Abstract:** The influence of three high frequency ABCB1 polymorphisms (c.1236C>T, c.2677G>A/T, and c.3435C>T) and the ABCG2 c.421C>A polymorphism on the disposition of doxorubicin in Asian breast cancer patients receiving adjuvant chemotherapy was investigated. The allelic frequency of the ABCB1 c.1236T, c.2677T, c.2677A, and c.3435T variants were 60%, 38%, 7%, and 22%, respectively, and the frequency of the ABCG2 c.421A allele was 23%. Pairwise analysis showed increased exposure levels to doxorubicin in patients harboring at least one ABCB1 c.1236T allele (P=0.03). Patients homozygous for the CC-GG-CC genotype had significantly lower doxorubicin exposure levels compared to patients who had CT-GT-CT (P=0.02) and TT-TT-TT genotypes (P=0.03). Significantly increased clearance of doxorubicin was also observed in patients harboring CC-GG-CC genotypes when compared to patients harboring the CT-GT-CT genotype (P=0.01). Patients harboring the CC-GG-CC genotypes had significantly lower peak plasma concentrations of doxorubicinol compared to patients who had TT-TT-TT genotypes (P=0.03). No significant influences on doxorubicin pharmacokinetic parameters were observed in relation to the ABCG2 c.421C>A polymorphism. The three high frequency linked polymorphisms in ABCB1 may influence doxorubicin disposition in Asian breast cancer patients.

**Key content:** This pharmacokinetic study enrolled Asian (predominantly Chinese, Malay, Indian) breast cancer patients receiving FAC (fluorouracil, doxorubicin, cyclophosphamide) or FEC (fluorouracil, epirubicin, cyclophosphamide) adjuvant chemotherapy. Plasma concentrations of doxorubicin and doxorubicinol were measured. DNA genotyping was performed for ABCB1 c.1236C>T (rs1128503), c.2677G>T/A (rs2032582), c.3435C>T (rs1045642), and ABCG2 c.421C>A (rs2231142). The three ABCB1 SNPs are in strong linkage disequilibrium in Asian populations (less than in Caucasians). Haplotype analysis of the three linked ABCB1 polymorphisms showed that the CC-GG-CC haplotype (wild-type) carriers had significantly lower AUC and higher clearance of doxorubicin compared to CT-GT-CT (P=0.02) and TT-TT-TT (P=0.03) haplotype carriers. Peak plasma concentrations (Cmax) of doxorubicinol were also significantly lower in CC-GG-CC homozygotes vs TT-TT-TT (P=0.03). The ABCG2 c.421C>A polymorphism showed no significant association with doxorubicin PK. The study suggested that the ABCB1 haplotype (rather than individual SNPs) is the key determinant of doxorubicin exposure in this population.

## Filename: PMC11786012.md

**Title:** Germline Human Leukocyte Antigen Status is Associated With Immunotherapy-Induced Pneumonitis and Treatment Response in Patients With Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer With High Programmed Death-Ligand 1 Expression

**Authors:** Alvin HK Cheung, Zeta Mui, Walter W Yeung, Chit Chow, Mandy F Yu, Olivia H Chen, Kit-Yee Wong, Fuda Xie, Yat Ming Lau, Alfred S-L Cheng, Wei Kang, Ka-Fai To, Tony S Mok, Molly SC Li
**Journal:** JTO Clinical and Research Reports
**Date:** 2024 Oct 23
**DOI:** 10.1016/j.jtocrr.2024.100754
**PMID:** 39897119
**PMCID:** PMC11786012

**Abstract:** The germline human leukocyte antigen (HLA) status has been found to be associated with immunotherapy outcomes in patients with NSCLC, but its correlation to immunotherapy-induced pneumonitis and prognostic impact in the Asian population remains largely unknown. We evaluated the HLA genotype of the germline and available tumor samples in 42 patients with programmed death-ligand 1 (PD-L1) expression ≥50% undergoing pembrolizumab immunotherapy. The HLA allele expression was correlated with tumor response, disease survival, and the occurrence of pneumonitis.

**Key content:** This retrospective study of 42 Asian (predominantly Hong Kong Chinese) NSCLC patients treated with first-line pembrolizumab (PD-L1 ≥50%) evaluated germline HLA genotyping. Key findings:

- **Pneumonitis:** 12 patients (28.6%) developed immunotherapy-induced pneumonitis. HLA-A*02:07 allele was significantly associated with an increased risk of pneumonitis (OR=6.05, 95% CI 1.39-26.34, P=0.016). Patients with HLA-A*02:07 had a 6-fold higher risk of pneumonitis.
- **Treatment response:** HLA-B*38:02 (P=0.029) and HLA-DQB1*03:01 (P=0.029) were significantly associated with objective response. Patients with HLA-DQA1*03:01/DQB1*03:01 haplotype were associated with better progression-free survival (PFS).
- **Heterozygosity advantage:** Greater HLA-I heterozygosity at HLA-A, -B, and -C loci was associated with better overall survival.
- **Tumor HLA:** Of 15 tumor samples analyzed, 6 (40%) had HLA-I copy number losses; no significant survival difference was observed for tumor HLA losses. TMB did not significantly differ among patients with different treatment outcomes.
- The study concluded that HLA-A*02:07 is a germline biomarker for pembrolizumab-induced pneumonitis and HLA-B*38:02 and HLA-DQB1*03:01 are associated with treatment response in Asian NSCLC patients.

## Filename: PMC2875867.md

**Title:** CYP2B6, CYP2A6 and UGT2B7 Genetic Polymorphisms are Predictors of Efavirenz Mid-dose Concentration in HIV-infected Patients

**Authors:** Awewura Kwara, Margaret Lartey, Kwamena WC Sagoe, Ernest Kenu, Michael H Court
**Journal:** AIDS (London, England)
**Date:** 2009 Oct 23
**DOI:** 10.1097/QAD.0b013e3283319908
**PMID:** 19779319
**PMCID:** PMC2875867

**Abstract:** UDP-glucuronosyltransferase (UGT) 2B7 was recently identified as the main enzyme mediating efavirenz N-glucuronidation. This study determined whether selected UGT2B7 polymorphisms could enhance the prediction of efavirenz plasma concentrations from CYP2B6 and CYP2A6 genotypes. Mid-dose efavirenz plasma concentrations were determined in 94 HIV-infected Ghanaian patients at 2–8 weeks of antiretroviral therapy. UGT2B7 exon 2 SNPs c.735A>G (UGT2B7*1c; rs28365062) and c.802C>T (H268Y; UGT2B7*2; rs7439366) were determined by direct sequencing.

**Key content:**
- Study population: 94 HIV-infected Ghanaian patients on efavirenz-based antiretroviral therapy (600 mg daily).
- CYP2B6 c.516TT genotype was the strongest predictor of high efavirenz levels, accounting for a 4,030 ng/mL increase (P<0.001).
- UGT2B7*1a carrier status (wildtype, non-carrier of *1c or *2) was independently associated with ~475 ng/mL increase in efavirenz (P=0.004).
- CYP2A6*9 and/or *17 carrier status was the third variable in the model, associated with 372 ng/mL increase (P=0.013).
- The combined multivariate model predicted efavirenz log concentrations using: Log10[EFV] = 3.068 + (0.648 × CYP2B6 c.516TT) + (0.148 × UGT2B7*1a) + (0.120 × CYP2A6*9 or *17).
- UGT2B7*2 carriers had lower efavirenz concentrations (median 1,562 ng/mL) compared to non-carriers (median 2,306 ng/mL; P=0.020).
- In univariate analysis, CYP2B6 c.516TT genotype (P<0.001), UGT2B7*1a (P=0.046), CYP2B6*9 (P=0.019), and CYP2A6*9/*17 (P=0.030) were significantly associated.
- The multi-gene model explained more pharmacokinetic variability than CYP2B6 genotype alone, validating UGT2B7 as a clinically relevant efavirenz-metabolizing enzyme gene.

## Filename: PMC3016221.md

**Title:** Genetic variants in the KIF6 region and coronary event reduction from statin therapy

**Authors:** Yonghong Li, Marc S Sabatine, Carmen H Tong, Ian Ford, Todd G Kirchgessner, Christopher J Packard, Michele Robertson, Charles M Rowland, Lance A Bare, James Shepherd, James J Devlin, Olga A Iakoubova
**Journal:** Human Genetics
**Date:** 2010 Oct 1
**DOI:** 10.1007/s00439-010-0892-6
**PMID:** 20886236

**Abstract:** Fine mapping of the KIF6 region was conducted to determine whether the previously reported rs20455 (Trp719Arg) association with differential coronary event reduction from statin therapy could be explained by another SNP and whether other independent variants predict statin response. - Six CYP2B6 SNPs genotyped: rs3211371, rs34097093, rs1042389, rs8192709, rs3745274, rs28399499.
- 168 ancestry informative markers (AIMs) used for population stratification correction.
- Rs3745274 (Q172H, CYP2B6*6): OR=3.61 per T allele (P=0.03) after PC adjustment. Without PC adjustment: OR=2.42 (P=0.07)—showing substantial confounding.

- rs2306283 allele frequency: 34% (G allele) in Caucasians, 75% in Asians.

**rs4149056 genotypic effects in Caucasians (vs TT reference):**
- CC genotype: pooled OR=2.9 (95% CI 1.59–5.34)
- TC genotype: pooled OR=1.6 (95% CI 1.20–2.16)
- TC+CC genotype: pooled OR=1.9 (95% CI 1.28–2.83)
- Simvastatin users: CC OR=2.8 (1.17–6.77), TC OR=1.8 (1.15–2.77)
- Atorvastatin users: CC OR=3.0 (1.02–8.93), TC OR=1.6 (1.07–2.33)

**Individual patient data (IPD) mixed-effects logit model (Caucasians):**
- CC vs TT: OR=3.8 (2.35–6.08, P<0.001)
- TC vs TT: OR=1.8 (1.38–2.42, P<0.001)

**rs2306283 effects:** No statistically significant association found in either Caucasians or Asians in genotype-based or IPD analysis.

- Additional findings: Higher statin dose associated with higher myopathy risk regardless of genotype.
- Conclusion: SLCO1B1 rs4149056 CC and TC genotypes significantly increase myopathy risk in statin users (especially simvastatin and atorvastatin); rs2306283 not significantly associated.

## Filename: PMC8376784.md

**Title:** Effect of SLCO1B1 T521C on Statin-related Myotoxicity with Use of Lovastatin and Atorvastatin

**Authors:** Brian Lu, Laura Sun, Manuel Seraydarian, Thomas J Hoffmann, Marisa W Medina, Carlos Iribarren, Ronald M Krauss, Neil Risch, Akinyemi Oni-Orisan
**Journal:** Clinical pharmacology and therapeutics
**Date:** 2021 Jul 23
**DOI:** 10.1002/cpt.2337
**PMID:** 34114646
**PMCID:** PMC8376784

**Abstract:** This study investigated whether SLCO1B1 rs4149056 genotype was associated with statin-induced myotoxicity in atorvastatin and lovastatin users using the GERA cohort. 233 cases (statin-induced myopathy+rhabdomyolysis) matched to 2,342 controls. Simvastatin CC vs TT: OR=4.6 (P=2×10−3). Lovastatin CC vs TT: OR=4.5 (P=1×10−3). Atorvastatin CC vs TT: OR=2.0 (P=0.3, not significant). All statins combined: CC vs TT OR=3.7 (P=1×10−5).

**Key content:**
- Study population: Kaiser Permanente Northern California (KPNC) GERA cohort; statin users with daily dose ≥40 mg.
- Cases: 233 statin-induced myopathy+rhabdomyolysis cases (defined as CK>5×ULN within 6 months of statin prescription, or ICD-9 rhabdomyolysis diagnosis).
- Controls: 2,342 matched controls (age, sex, statin type, statin dose matched). Median statin use: 1,000 days (cases), 1,040 days (controls).
- Distribution: Simvastatin n=82 cases/748 controls; Lovastatin n=85/901; Atorvastatin n=66/693.
- Demographics: ~72% EUR, ~12% LAT, ~9% EAS, ~7% AFR.

**Primary results (myopathy+rhabdomyolysis, CC vs TT):**
- Simvastatin: OR=4.62 (95% CI 1.58–11.9, P=0.002); TC vs TT: OR=1.78 (P=0.023). C allele frequency: 25% cases vs 15% controls (P=5×10−4).
- Lovastatin: OR=4.49 (95% CI 1.68–10.8, P=0.001); TC vs TT: OR=1.11 (P=0.715, NS). C allele: 19% cases vs 14% controls (P=8×10−4).
- Atorvastatin: OR=1.99 (95% CI 0.44–6.59, P=0.303, not significant); TC vs TT: OR=1.12 (P=0.716, NS). C allele: 17% cases vs 14% controls (P=0.40, NS).
- All statins combined: TC vs TT OR=1.4 (P=0.03); CC vs TT OR=3.7 (P=1×10−5).

**Secondary analysis (rhabdomyolysis alone):**
- All statins: CC vs TT OR=3.7 (95% CI 1.97–6.75, P=2×10−5); TC vs TT OR=1.3 (NS).
- Statin-type trends mirror primary analysis.

- Sensitivity analyses matching additionally on race/ethnicity and on obesity/diabetes yielded consistent results.
- This is the first study to examine SLCO1B1 and lovastatin-induced myotoxicity, establishing that the association extends to lovastatin. Evidence for atorvastatin was less clear (non-significant trend). CPIC guidelines currently recommend simvastatin dose adjustment for rs4149056 carriers; authors suggest considering lovastatin in updated guidelines.

## Filename: PMC8513493.md

**Title:** Genome wide association study identifies the common variants in CYP3A4 and CYP3A5 responsible for variation in tacrolimus trough concentration in Caucasian kidney transplant recipients

**Authors:** William S Oetting, Baolin Wu, David P Schladt, Weihua Guan, Rory P Remmel, Roslyn B Mannon, Arthur J Matas, Ajay K Israni, DeKAF Genomics Investigators
**Journal:** The pharmacogenomics journal
**Date:** 2017 Nov 21
**DOI:** 10.1038/tpj.2017.49
**PMID:** 29160300
**PMCID:** PMC8513493

**Abstract:** A GWAS was performed on TAC trough concentrations in 1,345 adult European-American (EA) kidney transplant recipients with two CYP3A5 loss-of-function alleles. Only CYP3A4*22 (rs35599367) was genome-wide significant. Together, CYP3A5*3 and CYP3A4*22 explained 16% of TAC trough variability; clinical variables explained 24.1%; the full model explained 40.1%. Low-frequency variants should be evaluated as potential additional contributors.

**Key content:**
- Study: DeKAF Genomics cohort; EA kidney transplant recipients; 25,255 TAC trough measurements over first 6 months.
- GWAS platform: custom exome-plus Affymetrix TxArray SNP chip (~782,000 markers; 1,221,308 after imputation).
- Analysis restricted to 1,345 EA recipients with 2 CYP3A5 LoF alleles (1,342 *3/*3; 2 *3/*6; 1 *3/*7), to isolate effects outside of CYP3A5.
- Key result: Only CYP3A4*22 (rs35599367, in intron 6, reduced CYP3A4 expression) was genome-wide significant (p=2.21×10−17; beta=0.300, SE=0.035). MAF=0.054 in this EA cohort.
- 11 additional SNPs in the CYP3A locus region (chromosome 7) were also significant but all became sub-threshold after adjustment for CYP3A4*22.
- Variance explained:
  - Clinical variables alone: 24.1%
  - Clinical variables + CYP3A5*3 (rs776746): 36.6%
  - Clinical variables + CYP3A4*22 (rs35599367): 28.5%
  - Clinical variables + both CYP3A5*3 + CYP3A4*22: 40.1%
  - Combined genetic contribution (CYP3A5*3 + CYP3A4*22): ~16% of total variance
- Other previously reported candidate genes (ABCB1, POR, CYP2C19, PPARA, ABCC8, SLC28A3) were not significant in this cohort.
- Conclusion: In EA patients with universal CYP3A5 loss-of-function, CYP3A4*22 is the only common variant with a meaningful effect on TAC troughs. The remaining ~60% of variance is unexplained, suggesting low-frequency variants detectable only by sequencing may be important.

## Filename: PMC11158672.md

**Title:** Influence of ABCB1 and ABCG2 polymorphisms on doxorubicin disposition in Asian breast cancer patients

**Authors:** Suman Lal, Zee Wan Wong, Edwin Sandanaraj, Xiaoqiang Xiang, Peter Cher Siang Ang, Edmund J D Lee, Balram Chowbay
**Journal:** Cancer Science
**Date:** 2008 Mar 28
**DOI:** 10.1111/j.1349-7006.2008.00744.x
**PMID:** 18377430
**PMCID:** PMC11158672

**Abstract:** The influence of three high frequency ABCB1 polymorphisms (c.1236C>T, c.2677G>A/T, and c.3435C>T) and the ABCG2 c.421C>A polymorphism on the disposition of doxorubicin in Asian breast cancer patients receiving adjuvant chemotherapy was investigated. The allelic frequency of the ABCB1 c.1236T, c.2677T, c.2677A, and c.3435T variants were 60%, 38%, 7%, and 22%, respectively, and the frequency of the ABCG2 c.421A allele was 23%. Pairwise analysis showed increased exposure levels to doxorubicin in patients harboring at least one ABCB1 c.1236T allele (P=0.03). Patients homozygous for the CC-GG-CC genotype had significantly lower doxorubicin exposure levels compared to patients who had CT-GT-CT (P=0.02) and TT-TT-TT genotypes (P=0.03). Significantly increased clearance of doxorubicin was also observed in patients harboring CC-GG-CC genotypes when compared to patients harboring the CT-GT-CT genotype (P=0.01). Patients harboring the CC-GG-CC genotypes had significantly lower peak plasma concentrations of doxorubicinol compared to patients who had TT-TT-TT genotypes (P=0.03). No significant influences on doxorubicin pharmacokinetic parameters were observed in relation to the ABCG2 c.421C>A polymorphism. The three high frequency linked polymorphisms in ABCB1 may influence doxorubicin disposition in Asian breast cancer patients.

**Key content:** This pharmacokinetic study enrolled Asian (predominantly Chinese, Malay, Indian) breast cancer patients receiving FAC (fluorouracil, doxorubicin, cyclophosphamide) or FEC (fluorouracil, epirubicin, cyclophosphamide) adjuvant chemotherapy. Plasma concentrations of doxorubicin and doxorubicinol were measured. DNA genotyping was performed for ABCB1 c.1236C>T (rs1128503), c.2677G>T/A (rs2032582), c.3435C>T (rs1045642), and ABCG2 c.421C>A (rs2231142). The three ABCB1 SNPs are in strong linkage disequilibrium in Asian populations (less than in Caucasians). Haplotype analysis of the three linked ABCB1 polymorphisms showed that the CC-GG-CC haplotype (wild-type) carriers had significantly lower AUC and higher clearance of doxorubicin compared to CT-GT-CT (P=0.02) and TT-TT-TT (P=0.03) haplotype carriers. Peak plasma concentrations (Cmax) of doxorubicinol were also significantly lower in CC-GG-CC homozygotes vs TT-TT-TT (P=0.03). The study suggested that the ABCB1 haplotype (rather than individual SNPs) is the key determinant of doxorubicin exposure in this population.

**Abstract:** The germline human leukocyte antigen (HLA) status has been found to be associated with immunotherapy outcomes in patients with NSCLC, but its correlation to immunotherapy-induced pneumonitis and prognostic impact in the Asian population remains largely unknown. **Abstract:** UDP-glucuronosyltransferase (UGT) 2B7 was recently identified as the main enzyme mediating efavirenz N-glucuronidation. This study determined whether selected UGT2B7 polymorphisms could enhance the prediction of efavirenz plasma concentrations from CYP2B6 and CYP2A6 genotypes. Mid-dose efavirenz plasma concentrations were determined in 94 HIV-infected Ghanaian patients at 2–8 weeks of antiretroviral therapy. UGT2B7 exon 2 SNPs c.735A>G (UGT2B7*1c; rs28365062) and c.802C>T (H268Y; UGT2B7*2; rs7439366) were determined by direct sequencing.

**Key content:**
- Study population: 94 HIV-infected Ghanaian patients on efavirenz-based antiretroviral therapy (600 mg daily).
- CYP2B6 c.516TT genotype was the strongest predictor of high efavirenz levels, accounting for a 4,030 ng/mL increase (P<0.001).
- UGT2B7*1a carrier status (wildtype, non-carrier of *1c or *2) was independently associated with ~475 ng/mL increase in efavirenz (P=0.004).
- CYP2A6*9 and/or *17 carrier status was the third variable in the model, associated with 372 ng/mL increase (P=0.013).

**Abstract:** Fine mapping of the KIF6 region was conducted to determine whether the previously reported rs20455 (Trp719Arg) association with differential coronary event reduction from statin therapy could be explained by another SNP and whether other independent variants predict statin response. Three tightly linked SNPs—rs20455, rs9462535, and rs9471077—were consistently associated with differential coronary event reduction from statin therapy across three prospective clinical trials.

**Key content:**
- Study population: Caucasian patients from CARE (2,913), PROVE IT-TIMI22 (1,817), and WOSCOPS (1,570) trials.
- Statins used: pravastatin 40 mg/day (CARE, WOSCOPS) and atorvastatin 80 mg vs. pravastatin 40 mg (PROVE IT-TIMI22).
- 170 SNPs in the KIF6 region (chr 6, 39.4–39.8 Mb) were tested.
- Three SNPs consistently associated with differential statin response across all three studies (all P_interaction < 0.1 dominant model): rs20455 (Trp719Arg missense), rs9462535 (intronic), rs9471077 (intronic). These three SNPs are in high LD (r²=0.84–0.98).
- Carriers of the 719Arg allele (CT+CC at rs20455; ~60% of European-ancestry population) showed significant coronary event reduction from statin therapy: CARE HR=0.67, P=0.0074; PROVE IT-TIMI22 OR=0.62, P=0.0003; WOSCOPS OR=0.50, P<0.0001. - The causal variant could not be definitively identified due to high LD; the missense Trp719Arg substitution (near kinesin cargo-binding domain) remains a candidate functional variant (BLOSUM62 score of -3 indicating an unlikely substitution).
- Conclusion: KIF6 Trp719Arg (rs20455) is the best representative of a tightly linked haplotype block associated with differential coronary event reduction from statins.

**Abstract:** CYP2B6 variation predicts pharmacokinetic characteristics of NNRTIs (efavirenz, nevirapine). The effect of CYP2B6 variation on virologic response to NNRTIs was explored in 91 HIV-infected women from the Women's Interagency HIV Study (WIHS). - Outcome: Virologic suppression (undetectable viral load) within ~54 weeks.
- Six CYP2B6 SNPs genotyped: rs3211371, rs34097093, rs1042389, rs8192709, rs3745274, rs28399499.
- 168 ancestry informative markers (AIMs) used for population stratification correction.
- Rs3745274 (Q172H, CYP2B6*6): OR=3.61 per T allele (P=0.03) after PC adjustment. Without PC adjustment: OR=2.42 (P=0.07)—showing substantial confounding.

- This was the first study to associate SLCO1B1 variants with ACE inhibitor-induced cough. Higher *15 frequency in Asians (12.3%) vs Caucasians (2.4%) may explain higher cough incidence in Chinese patients on ACEIs.

## Filename: PMC5728073.md

**Title:** Simvastatin intolerance genetic determinants: some features in ethnic Uzbek patients with coronary artery disease

**Authors:** Aleksandr B Shek, Ravshanbek D Kurbanov, Guzal J Abdullaeva, Aleksandr V Nagay, Shavkat U Hoshimov, Ulugbek I Nizamov, Adolat V Ziyaeva, Rano B Alieva
**Journal:** Archives of Medical Sciences. Atherosclerotic Diseases
**Date:** 2017 Oct 5
**DOI:** 10.5114/amsad.2017.70597
**PMID:** 29242847
**PMCID:** PMC5728073

**Abstract:** CYP3A5 (6986A>G), CYP2C9 (430C>T), CYP2C9 (1075A>C), SLCO1B1 (521T>C) and BCRP (ABCG2, 421C>A) gene polymorphisms were studied in 50 ethnic Uzbek CAD patients with simvastatin intolerance and 50 tolerant controls. CYP3A5 *3/*3 genotype and CA BCRP were associated with simvastatin-induced liver symptoms (P=0.0001). For muscle symptoms (SAMS, n=13), CYP3A5 *3/*3 (OR=8.6, P=0.003) and SLCO1B1 C allele (OR=3.54, P=0.017) were predominant.

**Key content:**
- Study population: 100 ethnic Uzbek patients with chronic CAD; case group n=50 (simvastatin intolerance); control group n=50 (tolerant to simvastatin 20-40 mg/day for ≥12 months).
- Drug: simvastatin 10-20 mg/day (case group); 20-40 mg/day (control group).
- Intolerance types: liver symptoms (transaminase >3×ULN, n=37) and statin-associated muscle symptoms (SAMS, CK>3×ULN, n=13).
- PCR-RFLP genotyping method.

**Results by intolerance type:**

*All 50 cases vs 50 controls (combined):*
- CYP3A5 *3/*3: cases 28/50 (56%), controls 6/50 (12%); OR=9.33, 95% CI 3.37–25.9, P<0.001.
- BCRP (ABCG2) 421C>A C carriers (CA): cases 19/50, controls 8/50; OR=3.22, P=0.024.
- CYP2C9*2 and CYP2C9*3: not significantly different.
- SLCO1B1 521T>C C carriers: cases 15/50, controls 14/50; OR=0.91, P=1.00 (not significant overall).

*Liver symptoms (n=37 cases vs n=50 controls):*
- CYP3A5 *3/*3: OR=9.63, 95% CI 3.29–28.1, P<0.001.
- BCRP CA: OR=7.7, 95% CI 2.83–20.9, P<0.001.
- SLCO1B1 C carriers: OR=1.67 (NS).

*Muscle symptoms/SAMS (n=13 cases vs n=50 controls):*
- CYP3A5 *3/*3: OR=8.56, 95% CI 2.14–34.1, P=0.003.
- SLCO1B1 C allele: OR=3.54, 95% CI 1.35–9.27, P=0.017.
- SLCO1B1 C carriers (genotype): OR=4.11, P=0.05 (borderline).
- BCRP: OR=2.33 (NS).

- Note: CYP2C9*2 allele was not in Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium in this Uzbek population (P<0.001), suggesting possible population-specific deviation.
- Conclusion: CYP3A5 *3/*3 + BCRP CA associated with simvastatin liver toxicity; CYP3A5 *3/*3 + SLCO1B1 C allele associated with simvastatin muscle toxicity in Uzbek patients with CAD.

## Filename: PMC7223759.md

**Title:** Association study of genetic polymorphisms in proteins involved in oseltamivir transport, metabolism, and interactions with adverse reactions in Mexican patients with acute respiratory diseases

**Authors:** Mario Bermúdez de León, Rafael B R León-Cachón, Beatriz Silva-Ramírez, Rosa Nelly González-Ríos, Brenda Escobedo-Guajardo, Roberto Leyva-Parra, Benjamín Tovar-Cisneros, Everardo González-González, Abdiel Alvarado-Díaz, Ofelia Vázquez-Monsiváis, Viviana Mata-Tijerina, Lorena Puente-Lugo, Erick Álvarez-Galván, María José Currás-Tuala, Miguel Aguado-Barrera, Fabiola Castorena-Torres, Juan Manuel Alcocer-González, Guillermo Elizondo, Ana María Salinas-Martínez
**Journal:** The Pharmacogenomics Journal
**Date:** 2020 Feb 4
**DOI:** 10.1038/s41397-020-0151-8
**PMID:** 32015454
**PMCID:** PMC7223759

**Abstract:** 310 Mexican patients with acute respiratory diseases suspected of influenza A/H1N1 were treated with oseltamivir 75 mg/day for 5 days and evaluated for adverse drug reactions (ADRs). ADRs occurred in 38 patients (12.25%): hypersensitivity (1.9%), gastritis (10%), depression/anxiety (0.9%). ABCB1-rs1045642 (C3435T) was significantly associated with ADRs under the recessive model (P=0.017); allele C was protective and allele T was associated with ADRs.

**Key content:**
- Retrospective study at IMSS clinics in Nuevo Leon, Mexico; sample collected December 2009–December 2012.
- Drug: oseltamivir phosphate 75 mg/kg/day for ≥5 days.
- Influenza confirmation: 130/310 (42%) positive for A/H1N1; 11 (3.5%) positive for seasonal influenza; 169 (54.5%) negative.
- ADRs: 38 patients (12.25%): gastritis n=31 (10%), hypersensitivity n=6 (1.9%), depression/anxiety n=3 (0.9%); no seizures or hallucinations.
- Genes/SNPs analyzed: ABCB1 (rs3789243, rs1128503, rs2032582, rs1045642), CES1 (rs71647871, rs2307243), SLC15A1 (rs2297322), NEU2 (rs2233385).
- SNPs not in Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium (excluded from further analysis): SLC15A1-rs2297322, ABCB1-rs2032582 (G/A variant), CES1-rs2307243.
- Association analysis results (codominant model): Only ABCB1-rs1045642 was significantly associated with ADRs (P=0.031).
- Recessive model analysis for gastritis: ABCB1-rs1045642 C allele is protective (OR=0.395, P=0.017); T allele = increased risk of gastritis (OR=2.532, 95% CI 1.154–5.560).
- Haplotype analysis: 4 major haplotypes identified; no significant association between haplotypes and ADRs.
- Linkage disequilibrium: ABCB1-rs3789243 and CES1-rs71647871 in LD (P=0.029); ABCB1-rs1045642 and NEU2-rs2233385 in LD (P=0.0435).
- Conclusion: ABCB1 rs1045642 (C3435T) is a potential predictive biomarker of ADRs in oseltamivir treatment; specifically, the T/T genotype (recessive) is associated with gastritis risk.

## Filename: PMC8159730.md

**Title:** A systematic review and meta-analysis of genotype-based and individualized data analysis of SLCO1B1 gene and statin-induced myopathy

**Authors:** Saowalak Turongkaravee, Jiraphun Jittikoon, Thitiya Lukkunaprasit, Sermsiri Sangroongruangsri, Usa Chaikledkaew, Ammarin Thakkinstian
**Journal:** The Pharmacogenomics Journal
**Date:** 2021 Feb 19
**DOI:** 10.1038/s41397-021-00208-w
**PMID:** 33608664
**PMCID:** PMC8159730

**Abstract:** Meta-analysis determining genotypic effects of SLCO1B1 rs4149056 and rs2306283 on myopathy in statin patients. Fourteen studies pooled for rs4149056; minor C allele frequency 15% in Caucasians and 14% in Asians. Six studies pooled for rs2306283; minor G allele frequency 34% in Caucasians and 75% in Asians. In Caucasians, CC genotype: OR=2.9 (95% CI 1.59–5.34); TC genotype: OR=1.6 (95% CI 1.20–2.16) vs TT. Simvastatin users: CC OR=2.8, TC OR=1.8. Atorvastatin users: CC OR=3.0, TC OR=1.6.

**Key content:**
- Systematic review methodology: PubMed, EMBASE, Cochrane, CINAHL, SCOPUS, Thai databases; studies through 2019.
- Two independent reviewers; risk of bias assessed.
- 14 studies included for rs4149056 meta-analysis; 6 studies for rs2306283.
- Population: predominantly Caucasian (14 studies) and some Asian data.
- rs4149056 allele frequency: 15% (C allele) in Caucasians, 14% in Asians.
- rs2306283 allele frequency: 34% (G allele) in Caucasians, 75% in Asians.

**rs4149056 genotypic effects in Caucasians (vs TT reference):**
- CC genotype: pooled OR=2.9 (95% CI 1.59–5.34)
- TC genotype: pooled OR=1.6 (95% CI 1.20–2.16)
- TC+CC genotype: pooled OR=1.9 (95% CI 1.28–2.83)
- Simvastatin users: CC OR=2.8 (1.17–6.77), TC OR=1.8 (1.15–2.77)
- Atorvastatin users: CC OR=3.0 (1.02–8.93), TC OR=1.6 (1.07–2.33)

**Individual patient data (IPD) mixed-effects logit model (Caucasians):**
- CC vs TT: OR=3.8 (2.35–6.08, P<0.001)
- TC vs TT: OR=1.8 (1.38–2.42, P<0.001)

**rs2306283 effects:** No statistically significant association found in either Caucasians or Asians in genotype-based or IPD analysis.

- Additional findings: Higher statin dose associated with higher myopathy risk regardless of genotype.
- Conclusion: SLCO1B1 rs4149056 CC and TC genotypes significantly increase myopathy risk in statin users (especially simvastatin and atorvastatin); rs2306283 not significantly associated.

## Filename: PMC8376784.md

**Title:** Effect of SLCO1B1 T521C on Statin-related Myotoxicity with Use of Lovastatin and Atorvastatin

**Authors:** Brian Lu, Laura Sun, Manuel Seraydarian, Thomas J Hoffmann, Marisa W Medina, Carlos Iribarren, Ronald M Krauss, Neil Risch, Akinyemi Oni-Orisan
**Journal:** Clinical pharmacology and therapeutics
**Date:** 2021 Jul 23
**DOI:** 10.1002/cpt.2337
**PMID:** 34114646
**PMCID:** PMC8376784

**Abstract:** This study investigated whether SLCO1B1 rs4149056 genotype was associated with statin-induced myotoxicity in atorvastatin and lovastatin users using the GERA cohort. 233 cases (statin-induced myopathy+rhabdomyolysis) matched to 2,342 controls. Simvastatin CC vs TT: OR=4.6 (P=2×10−3). Lovastatin CC vs TT: OR=4.5 (P=1×10−3). Atorvastatin CC vs TT: OR=2.0 (P=0.3, not significant). All statins combined: CC vs TT OR=3.7 (P=1×10−5).

**Key content:**
- Study population: Kaiser Permanente Northern California (KPNC) GERA cohort; statin users with daily dose ≥40 mg.
- Cases: 233 statin-induced myopathy+rhabdomyolysis cases (defined as CK>5×ULN within 6 months of statin prescription, or ICD-9 rhabdomyolysis diagnosis).
- Controls: 2,342 matched controls (age, sex, statin type, statin dose matched). Median statin use: 1,000 days (cases), 1,040 days (controls).
- Distribution: Simvastatin n=82 cases/748 controls; Lovastatin n=85/901; Atorvastatin n=66/693.
- Demographics: ~72% EUR, ~12% LAT, ~9% EAS, ~7% AFR.

**Primary results (myopathy+rhabdomyolysis, CC vs TT):**
- Simvastatin: OR=4.62 (95% CI 1.58–11.9, P=0.002); TC vs TT: OR=1.78 (P=0.023). C allele frequency: 25% cases vs 15% controls (P=5×10−4).
- Lovastatin: OR=4.49 (95% CI 1.68–10.8, P=0.001); TC vs TT: OR=1.11 (P=0.715, NS). C allele: 19% cases vs 14% controls (P=8×10−4).
- Atorvastatin: OR=1.99 (95% CI 0.44–6.59, P=0.303, not significant); TC vs TT: OR=1.12 (P=0.716, NS). C allele: 17% cases vs 14% controls (P=0.40, NS).
- All statins combined: TC vs TT OR=1.4 (P=0.03); CC vs TT OR=3.7 (P=1×10−5).

**Secondary analysis (rhabdomyolysis alone):**
- All statins: CC vs TT OR=3.7 (95% CI 1.97–6.75, P=2×10−5); TC vs TT OR=1.3 (NS).
- Statin-type trends mirror primary analysis.

- Sensitivity analyses matching additionally on race/ethnicity and on obesity/diabetes yielded consistent results.
- This is the first study to examine SLCO1B1 and lovastatin-induced myotoxicity, establishing that the association extends to lovastatin. Evidence for atorvastatin was less clear (non-significant trend). CPIC guidelines currently recommend simvastatin dose adjustment for rs4149056 carriers; authors suggest considering lovastatin in updated guidelines.

## Filename: PMC8513493.md

**Title:** Genome wide association study identifies the common variants in CYP3A4 and CYP3A5 responsible for variation in tacrolimus trough concentration in Caucasian kidney transplant recipients

**Authors:** William S Oetting, Baolin Wu, David P Schladt, Weihua Guan, Rory P Remmel, Roslyn B Mannon, Arthur J Matas, Ajay K Israni, DeKAF Genomics Investigators
**Journal:** The pharmacogenomics journal
**Date:** 2017 Nov 21
**DOI:** 10.1038/tpj.2017.49
**PMID:** 29160300
**PMCID:** PMC8513493

**Abstract:** A GWAS was performed on TAC trough concentrations in 1,345 adult European-American (EA) kidney transplant recipients with two CYP3A5 loss-of-function alleles. Only CYP3A4*22 (rs35599367) was genome-wide significant. Together, CYP3A5*3 and CYP3A4*22 explained 16% of TAC trough variability; clinical variables explained 24.1%; the full model explained 40.1%. Low-frequency variants should be evaluated as potential additional contributors.

**Key content:**
- Study: DeKAF Genomics cohort; EA kidney transplant recipients; 25,255 TAC trough measurements over first 6 months.
- GWAS platform: custom exome-plus Affymetrix TxArray SNP chip (~782,000 markers; 1,221,308 after imputation).
- Analysis restricted to 1,345 EA recipients with 2 CYP3A5 LoF alleles (1,342 *3/*3; 2 *3/*6; 1 *3/*7), to isolate effects outside of CYP3A5.
- Key result: Only CYP3A4*22 (rs35599367, in intron 6, reduced CYP3A4 expression) was genome-wide significant (p=2.21×10−17; beta=0.300, SE=0.035). MAF=0.054 in this EA cohort.
- 11 additional SNPs in the CYP3A locus region (chromosome 7) were also significant but all became sub-threshold after adjustment for CYP3A4*22.
- Variance explained:
  - Clinical variables alone: 24.1%
  - Clinical variables + CYP3A5*3 (rs776746): 36.6%
  - Clinical variables + CYP3A4*22 (rs35599367): 28.5%
  - Clinical variables + both CYP3A5*3 + CYP3A4*22: 40.1%
  - Combined genetic contribution (CYP3A5*3 + CYP3A4*22): ~16% of total variance
- Other previously reported candidate genes (ABCB1, POR, CYP2C19, PPARA, ABCC8, SLC28A3) were not significant in this cohort.
- Conclusion: In EA patients with universal CYP3A5 loss-of-function, CYP3A4*22 is the only common variant with a meaningful effect on TAC troughs. The remaining ~60% of variance is unexplained, suggesting low-frequency variants detectable only by sequencing may be important.
